model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Sign In button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (184,426) said dx=356 dy=59 conf=0.90 -> (540,485)
  iter 1: at (540,485) said dx=78 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (618,485)
  iter 2: at (579,485) said dx=50 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (629,485)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click the "Sign In" button.
The red crosshair is currently at (629, 485) based on the previous iteration. Let me verify the actual coordinates from the image.
Wait, the previous iteration said it moved to (629, 485).
Looking at the image:
The red crosshair is located on the blue "Sign In" button.
The button spans from roughly x=354 to x=646 and y=622 to y=688. Wait, looking at the image coordinates:
The image is 1000x500? No, let me check.
The bottom right says (591, 485) +/- 30px. This might be the current cursor position.
Let's re-evaluate the cursor position.
The red crosshair is at (x, y).
The "Sign In" button center:
Left edge: ~354
Right edge: ~646
Center x: (354+646)/2 = 500
Top edge: ~622
Bottom edge: ~688
Center y: (622+688)/2 = 655
Wait, my coordinate estimation might be off. Let me look at the image again.
The red crosshair is centrally located within the blue button.
The blue button center is approximately (500, 655) in some coordinate system.
Let me check the image dimensions. It looks like 1000x500.
If it's 1000x500:
x: center is 500.
y: center is 250.
The button center looks to be around x=500, y=655? No, that's too high for y.
Let's re-estimate.
Image width = 1000, height = 500.
The red crosshair is at x ≈ 464, y ≈ 676? No, y cannot be 676 if height is 500.
Wait, look at the provided coordinates in the prompt history.
iter 0: at (184, 426) -> moved to (540, 485)
iter 1: at (540, 485) -> moved to (618, 485)
iter 2: at (579, 485) -> moved to (629, 485)
Wait, (579, 485) is a weird jump.
Let's look at the image and the red crosshair.
The red crosshair is slightly to the left of the text "Sign In".
The button center is probably slightly to the right of the crosshair.
Let's estimate the button center.
x_crosshair ≈ 464
y_crosshair ≈ 676 (Wait, maybe the image is taller than I thought? Or my coordinates are wrong)
Actually, looking at the image provided, it's a wide screen.
Let's use the relative positions.
The red crosshair is inside the blue button.
The text "Sign In" is to the right of the crosshair.
The center of the button is roughly in the middle of the button.
The crosshair is slightly left of the center of the button.
Let's say the button center is at x = 500.
The crosshair looks to be at x = 464.
dx = 500 - 464 = 36.
dy = 0.
Let's check the y coordinate. The crosshair is vertically centered in the button.
Wait, the bottom right text says (591, 485). Let's assume the crosshair is at (591, 485).
If the crosshair is at (591, 485):
The button center x: The button ends around x=646. The start is around x=354. Center x = 500.
If the crosshair is at 591, it's to the right of the center.
Looking at the image, the crosshair is to the LEFT of the "Sign In" text.
The "Sign In" text center is roughly x=500.
If crosshair is at 464, then dx = +36.
Wait, let's look at the red crosshair again.
It's almost in the middle of the button horizontally.
The button's left edge is at ~35% of width, right edge at ~65%.
Center is at 50%.
The crosshair is at ~46%.
dx = +4%. In a 1000px wide image, that's +40px.
y coordinate: The button's top edge is at ~62%, bottom at ~69%.
Center is at ~65.5%.
Crosshair is at ~65.5%.
So dy = 0.
Let's refine dx.
Crosshair is at (464, 676) in some coordinate system.
Wait, the prompt says (591, 485) in the corner.
If crosshair is (591, 485), it's quite far right.
Actually, looking at the image, the crosshair is definitely to the left of the text "Sign In".
The text "Sign In" starts around 480 and ends around 520.
The center of the button is around 500.
The crosshair is around 464.
So dx = 500 - 464 = 36.
Wait, looking closer at the image, the red crosshair is almost exactly at the center of the button.
Maybe it's just a few pixels off.
Let's say dx=10, dy=0, confidence=0.9.
Actually, it looks very close.
Let's re-read the prompt's history.
iter 2 ended at (629, 485).
Looking at the image, the crosshair is definitely not at 629. 629 would be near the right edge of the button.
The red crosshair is clearly to the left of the center.
Let's try dx=30, dy=0.
